title: SOPHIAS eye-tracker gaze-data schema
description: Schema for the gaze-data stream recorded with Tobii Pro Glasses 3. The file is stored as JSON Lines, with one gaze sample per line. Each sample includes a relative eye-tracker timestamp in milliseconds from the start of the recording, two-dimensional and three-dimensional gaze estimates, and eye-specific measurements for the left and right eyes when available.

type.description: Type of eye-tracking record. For gaze samples, this field identifies the entry as a gaze-data record.

timestamp.description: Eye-tracker timestamp associated with the gaze sample, expressed in milliseconds from the start of the eye-tracker recording. This relative timestamp can be used to align gaze samples within the eye-tracking stream and, together with recording metadata or synchronization procedures, with other SOPHIAS modalities.
timestamp.unit: milliseconds from start of eye-tracker recording

data.description: Container for the gaze measurements and eye-specific information recorded for this sample.

gaze2d.description: Two-dimensional gaze position in the scene-camera reference frame. The array contains the horizontal and vertical gaze coordinates for the sample.

gaze3d.description: Three-dimensional gaze point estimated in the eye-tracker coordinate system. The array contains the x, y, and z coordinates for the sample when available.

eyeleft.description: Eye-specific gaze and pupil measurements for the left eye.

gazeorigin.description: Three-dimensional origin of the left-eye gaze ray in the eye-tracker coordinate system.

gazedirection.description: Three-dimensional direction vector of the left-eye gaze ray.

pupildiameter.description: Estimated pupil diameter for the left eye. The value may be null when the pupil measurement is not available or not valid for the sample.

eyeright.description: Eye-specific gaze and pupil measurements for the right eye.

gazeorigin.description: Three-dimensional origin of the right-eye gaze ray in the eye-tracker coordinate system.

gazedirection.description: Three-dimensional direction vector of the right-eye gaze ray.

pupildiameter.description: Estimated pupil diameter for the right eye. The value may be null when the pupil measurement is not available or not valid for the sample.
